description_long: This guard stone is a robust, conical structure composed primarily of weathered stone, featuring two distinct horizontal bands of dark, metallic-looking trim. The upper section is adorned with yellow spiral motifs, while the lower section displays a large, intricate yellow emblem resembling a stylized bird or mythical creature. The midsection contains a circular, recessed black element encircled by a metallic ring, giving the appearance of a viewing port or decorative medallion. The surface shows signs of aging, with visible cracks and patches of mossy green discoloration, especially near the base. The overall form is solid and imposing, designed to serve as a protective barrier in urban environments.
description: A conical, stone guard stone with yellow decorative motifs, metallic bands, and a central circular medallion, showing signs of weathering and moss.
description_view_0: Front view showing yellow spiral motifs on the upper section, a central black medallion, and a large yellow emblem on the lower section.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the object's tapered profile, metallic bands, and visible cracks, with minimal decorative detail.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but with a different yellow emblem and less pronounced spiral motifs, still showing the central medallion.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with visible cracks and the layered structure but no major decorative features.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, at the entrance to driveways, or near the edges of sidewalks to protect buildings and pedestrians from vehicle encroachment.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright and possibly anchored to the pavement or ground surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface cracks, chipping along the lower edges, and areas of discoloration and moss growth near the base, likely caused by vehicle contact, weather exposure, and occasional foot traffic.
attribute_historical_markings: Decorative yellow spiral motifs and a large emblem resembling a stylized bird or mythical creature, painted on the upper and lower sections; no inscriptions or engravings.
